articles.title: Health Ministry investigates suspected Ebola case after traveler develops symptoms
articles.description: Israel’s Health Ministry is investigating a suspected Ebola case after a traveler returning from the Democratic Republic of Congo developed fever and headache.

articles.description: The difference between adequate vision care and exceptional vision care often comes down to one thing: personalization. At Insight Eye Care in Williamsville, the philosophy centers on treating each patient as an individual with unique visual needs, lifestyle demands and...

articles.title: Africa CDC chief says continent needs to invest own funds in Ebola response, vaccine
articles.description: African officials must step up financing to respond and develop vaccines for the Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda

articles.title: Buffalo Science Museum hosts annual Community Health Day event
articles.description: BUFFALO, N.Y. (WIVB) -- Juneteenth was a busy day at the Buffalo Museum of Science, where families attended a free health and wellness fair on Friday. Community Health Day is designed to connect visitors with local organizations while also encouraging people to learn more about health and wellness. The museum even waived admission feeds for [...]

articles.title: Montgomery gym encourages mental and physical exercises to improve long-term health
articles.description: Gerri Schneider felt like a spectator in her own life. Until Activate Brain & Body helped her reclaim health and happiness.

articles.title: NHS splashed almost £100m prescribing items such as bread and paracetamol last year
articles.description: THE NHS blew almost £100million prescribing items such as bread last year, a Sun probe found. The health service also dished out 15.7million packs of paracetamol for £64.4million — despite it being available in shops for as little as 29p. Other items include 292,600 loaves of gluten-free bread, 12,600 tubes of toothpaste and 664,700 prescriptions...

articles.title: Manitoba adding capacity for 200 more hip and knee replacement surgeries: health minister
articles.description: Manitoba's health minister says the government is upping orthopedic surgery capacity by adding 200 more hip and knee replacement surgeries at health-care facilities across the province.

articles.title: Tulsi Gabbard: Fauci lied to Congress, directed Wuhan research funding
articles.description: TULSI GABBARD says FAUCI lied: "The COVID pandemic caused tremendous hardship and pain for millions of our fellow Americans and countless people around the world. Now, after years of lies and censorship and cover-ups, the American people deserve transparency, truth and accountability."

articles.title: New Trigger for Alzheimer's Disease May Have Been Found
articles.description: Evidence suggests the earliest changes may begin deep inside neurons, pointing researchers toward a new piece of the puzzle.

articles.title: Lyme disease cases reported in Waterloo Region
articles.description: Region of Waterloo Public Health is reporting two human cases of Lyme disease, just days after issuing a warning that infected ticks had been discovered in the community.

articles.title: Dr. Crandall: Watermelon Boosts Heart Health, Hydration
articles.description: As temperatures rise, watermelon becomes a staple at picnics, barbecues, and poolside gatherings. According to cardiologist Dr. Chauncey Crandall, the summertime favorite offers more than just a refreshing way to cool off. "It's a superfood for the summer."

articles.title: Scientists find mechanism behind bird flu infections in dairy cattle
articles.description: When H5N1 bird flu first began infecting U.S. cattle in early 2024, diagnosis was elusive, because in cows, the disease looked completely different.

articles.title: Zebrafish study links altered neuronal genes to hyperarousal states
articles.description: The altered presence of tiny fragments of neuronal genes, called microexons, causes hyperarousal in zebrafish. This is the main conclusion of an international study led by the Pompeu Fabra University (UPF) and the Centre for Genomic Regulation (CRG).

articles.title: Confronted podcast shares Nelson father’s raw story of stillbirth and surviving deep grief
articles.description: Matt Earle’s daughter Mackenzie was stillborn at full term in February 2024.

articles.title: ReVital Cancer Rehabilitation helps patients rebuild strength, restore independence after diagnosis
articles.description: A cancer diagnosis can change everything, but ReVital Cancer Rehabilitation helps people from diagnosis through survivorship rebuild strength and restore independence.

articles.title: Wellness influencer died of ‘eminently treatable’ home birth complication that rarely kills in hospitals
articles.description: Intensive care paramedics couldn't detect her blood pressure and identified she'd had a postpartum hemorrhage, or excessive bleeding after birth. It happens in 1-5% of births.

articles.title: The Ottawa Hospital changing how ER patients are triaged
articles.description: The Ottawa Hospital says it is changing the way patients are triaged at its General and Civic campus emergency departments.

articles.title: As homeopathy sector retreats, even an industry giant is turning to dietary supplements instead
articles.description: In recent years, countries that once financed the pseudotreatments, such as the United Kingdom and France, have stopped doing so, and Germany has plans to do the same this year

articles.title: Cholesterol will go down if you eat 2 foods that work 'just like statins'
articles.description: High cholesterol is a common condition that affects millions of people in the UK and can lead to serious health complications if left untreated

articles.title: Caffeine sensitivity driving shift towards earlier consumption and better sleep habits
articles.description: Growing awareness of caffeine sensitivity is prompting more consumers to rethink when and how much caffeine they consume, as experts highlight its impact on sleep, anxiety and overall wellbeing.

articles.title: Think you're eating healthy? You may be missing this heart-protecting nutrient
articles.description: Eating five servings of fruits and vegetables may not be enough if you're missing foods rich in flavanols, a group of compounds linked to better heart health. Researchers found that choices like blackberries, plums, apples, broad beans, cherries, and green tea can dramatically increase flavanol intake.

articles.title: DWP confirms full list of 178 PIP conditions as claimants hit 4 million
articles.description: It's for people who have a long-term physical or mental health condition or disability or difficulty doing certain everyday tasks or getting around because of your condition.

articles.title: I was the healthiest I'd ever been... but then I had TWO heart attacks at just 26
articles.description: At 26, Channing Muller was the healthiest she'd ever been - she was athletic, a vegetarian and had no family history of heart disease - but still had two heart attacks.

articles.title: Why eat junk on a camping trip? Tips for nutritious food on the trail
articles.description: Getting outside generally improves mental and physical health. But too often, campers and hikers resort to the convenience of processed foods.

articles.title: Dh20m boost for Dubai’s first integrated cancer hospital infrastructure
articles.description: Dh20m boost powers advanced infrastructure at Dubai’s first integrated cancer hospital, enhancing life safety, reliability and cutting-edge cancer care.

articles.title: Donald Trump sparks fresh health concerns after 'struggling' with basic task
articles.description: The President sparked fresh concerns about his wellbeing after he appeared to struggle to fasten the Medal of Honor to US Army veteran Major Nicholas Dockery

articles.title: Tick season is getting worse. Can managing deer help?
articles.description: Ticks can cause serious diseases, but the tools for controlling them lag decades behind mosquitoes. In the northeast, health officials and researchers hope that efforts to control deer populations -- which serve as "party buses" for mating ticks -- along with new experimental approaches, can start to reverse the tide of ticks and the illnesses they cause.

articles.title: U.S. opens tariff probe targeting Germany’s drug pricing policies
articles.description: U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer said Germany's proposal to reduce spending on medicines was "a serious step backwards."

articles.title: FDA committee unanimously recommends first mRNA flu vaccine
articles.description: The Food and Drug Administration's top vaccine advisory committee voted Thursday to recommend an mRNA influenza vaccine. It's the first flu vaccine that uses the same technology as COVID-19 shots.

articles.title: Twins conjoined at head ‘defy all odds’ as tots are separated by Brit surgeons in ‘groundbreaking’ 40hrs of operations
articles.description: CONJOINED twin sisters have been separated by British surgeons after 40 hours of "groundbreaking" operations in the United Arab Emirates. Mercy and Goodness were born with their skulls fused together which caused their brain tissue and blood vessels to mix. The conjoined twins, born in southwest Nigeria, underwent the miracle 12-hour surgery at just 19...

articles.title: NHS free prescriptions for over-60s update after call for new age threshold
articles.description: The Department of Health and Social Care said there were no immediate plans to change the eligibility criteria for prescriptions.

articles.title: Opinion: Maternity deserts aren’t accidents. They’re the result of a design flaw
articles.description: Shrinking fertility should mean less strain on doctors. But nearly half of U.S. counties lack a practicing obstetrician or gynecologist.

articles.title: Opinion: STAT+: The real work for making dramatic gains against pancreatic cancer is just beginning
articles.description: The co-founders of Darwin Health write that the real work for making dramatic gains against pancreatic cancers is just beginning.

articles.title: ‘Bum-Maxxing’ Encourages Men to Be Their Worst Selves. Experts Say That’s Obviously a Problem.
articles.description: The internet’s “maxxing” era has given us sleep-maxxing, looks-maxxing, and enough wellness optimization content to make a person want to lie down. The newest entry skips self-improvement entirely and goes straight to performing emotional unavailability. Yikes. “Bum-maxxing” is a dating trend circulating online in which men deliberately act emotionally unavailable, tell women they’re busy, and [...] The post ‘Bum-Maxxing’ Encourages Men to Be Their Worst Selves. Experts Say That’s Obviously a Problem. appeared first on VICE .

articles.title: Urgent warning as six Victorians suffer liver poisoning from illegal weight loss peptide
articles.description: The Victorian Chief Health Officer said it was believed the patients became seriously unwell after using an unapproved peptide product labelled as Retatrutide.

articles.title: Indian entertainment is structurally shifting: Anand Rathi
articles.description: Omar Hinduja, Senior Analyst of Media and Entertainment at Anand Rathi, explains how India's entertainment landscape is rapidly developing - expanding its reach globally as it shifts to non-traditional media.

articles.title: Rehab scared me out of my wits – now I work there
articles.description: A former alcohol addict spoke out as a survey is launched to help others connect with support services.

articles.title: Iran deal may become ‘JCPOA 2.0,’ says analyst
articles.description: Adel Abdel Ghafar, senior fellow at the Australian Strategic Policy Institute, says Iran’s leadership is in a stronger position after surviving the recent conflict with the U.S. He says negotiations are now likely to center on a new nuclear arrangement resembling a “JCPOA 2.0,” placing limits on Iran’s enrichment while allowing a civilian program under international oversight.

articles.title: Has China's 618 shopping festival fallen out of fashion?
articles.description: Jacob Cooke, CEO of e-commerce consultancy WPIC, shares his takeaways the 618 shopping festival in China. He expects sales to grow around 11% this year, driven by a combination of an increase in e-commerce sales and an extended shopping period.

articles.title: Drivers urged to fill their cars with petrol or diesel before 8pm Tuesday
articles.description: The UK Health Security Agency, or UKHSA, and Met Office have issued yellow or amber heat-health alerts until June 233

articles.title: ICO watchdog opens inquiry into cameras in mental health patients’ bedrooms
articles.description: Exclusive: Oxevision system used by 40% of NHS mental health trusts being scrutinised by information commissioner over privacy concerns The information commissioner has launched an investigation into a controversial camera-based system for monitoring patients in their bedrooms, used by 40% of NHS mental health trusts, over data protection concerns. Oxevision is described by patients as “creepy” and a form of “spying” , and has been blamed by a bereaved mother for contributing to her daughter’s sense of paranoia before she took her own life. In the UK and Ireland, Samaritans can be contacted on freephone 116 123. In the US, you can call or text the National Suicide Prevention Lifeline on 988, chat on 988lifeline.org , or text HOME to 741741 to connect with a crisis counselor. In Australia, the crisis support service Lifeline is 13 11 14. Other international helplines can be found at befrienders.org Continue reading...

articles.title: Zealand Pharma stock suffered its two worst days on record. Here's what's next for the weight loss drugmaker
articles.description: While the market's enthusiasm for one of Zealand's experimental drugs has cooled, investors are shifting focus to amylin-based medicines as a growth driver

articles.title: London Marathon 2027 will be a two-day event, allowing 100,000 runners to cross the finish line in a unique double-format race
articles.description: The London Marathon is going to be a two-day event, which will allow the race to accommodate 100,000 runners. Here's how it's going to work.

articles.title: How Sebastian Prentice Built More Than a Gym
articles.description: Sebastian Prentice transformed Horizen from a small fitness group into a thriving community, where personal connections and shared values shape the experience beyond just exercise.

articles.title: China will restock their oil reserves, but it might take longer than some expect: Analyst
articles.description: Trivium China's Cory Combs, says oil demand in China is facing a structural plateau, given declining transportation-driven demand that cannot be offset by growing demand for industrial chemicals. He also discusses the nuances between China's commercial and strategic petroleum reserves, as well as the loss of China's monopsony on sanctioned global crude.
